2011-10-01.title: Al-Awlaki Killed in Drone Strike
2011-10-01.abstract: Anwar Al-Awlaki was killed in an American drone strike on his vehicle on Friday.

2011-10-01.abstract: September 30, 2011 - An American drone attack in Yemen kills United States-born Qaeda leader and key propagandist, Anwar al-Awlaki.

2011-10-01.title: TimesCast | Al-Awlaki's Influence
2011-10-01.abstract: September 30, 2011 - A look at Al-Awlaki's radicalizing influence on young Muslims in the English-speaking world.

2011-10-01.abstract: An effort to close the only center in North America where addicts may legally inject drugs was thwarted by Canada’s Supreme Court in a unanimous decision released Friday.

2011-10-01.abstract: Teachers in Acapulco have agreed to go back to work on Monday after more than a month on strike in protest over crime.

2011-10-02.abstract: Any immediate resumption of peace negotiations with the Palestinians appeared unlikely as the Israelis and Palestinians differed sharply over the terms.

2011-10-02.abstract: Veteran border crossers present an enormous challenge to American policymakers, because they continue to head north despite deterrents more severe than at any time in recent history.

2011-10-03.abstract: The Greek government also finalized a draft budget for 2012 and conceded that it would miss a deficit-reduction target.

2011-10-03.title: Americans Move Against Haqqani Network in Afghanistan
2011-10-03.abstract: The insurgent group, based in Pakistan’s northwestern frontier, flows fighters into Afghanistan and has orchestrated a long campaign of guerrilla and terrorist attacks against the Afghan government and its American sponsors. It has also harbored Al Qaeda and other terrorist groups.

2011-10-03.abstract: The Pentagon plans to pull most forces from Afghanistan by 2014. As they try to prepare for that exit, American units are focusing on the Haqqani insurgent network.

2011-10-04.abstract: The assassination of Burhanuddin Rabbani has laid bare the lack of a national consensus on what shape a reconciliation process and peace deal should take.

2011-10-04.abstract: Dr. Steinman and two others were honored for discovering the essential steps in the immune system’s response to infection.

2011-10-05.abstract: Mr. Porat opposed the removal of Jews from any land in exchange for peace with Israel’s neighbors.

2011-10-26.title: Nusrat Bhutto, Political Force in Pakistan, Dies at 82
2011-10-26.abstract: The mother and wife of prime ministers was an important figure in her own right.

2011-10-27.title: Rebels Said to Find Qaddafi Tie in Plot Against Iraq
2011-10-27.abstract: Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi may have been backing a Baathist-led coup against the Iraqi government.

2011-10-27.title: Officers Get 7 Years for Killing That Helped Inspire Egypt’s Revolt
2011-10-27.abstract: Supporters of Khaled Said said the seven-year sentence of the police officers responsible for his death was evidence that Egypt’s ruling military council was not committed to democratic changes.

2011-10-27.title: China Reins In Entertainment and Blogging
2011-10-27.abstract: Communist leaders are proposing some of the tightest limits on media and Internet freedoms in years.

2011-10-27.title: Iran’s Power Struggle Goes Beyond Personalities to Future of Presidency Itself
2011-10-27.abstract: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s remarks about a parliamentary system in Iran were seen as the latest blow in his battle with President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad.

2011-10-27.title: George L. Sherry, Envoy and Well-Known Voice at U.N., Dies at 87
2011-10-27.abstract: In the years just after the founding of the United Nations in 1945, Mr. Sherry became known as the English-speaking voice of Andrei Y. Vishinsky, the Soviet delegate.

2011-10-28.title: Greek Prime Minister Praises European Debt Deal
2011-10-28.abstract: Officials said a deal in which banks agreed to a 50 percent loss in Greek debt would allow Greece to make reforms without worrying about its debt burden.

2011-10-28.title: For Italy, Berlusconi Is a Problem but Also a Solution
2011-10-28.abstract: Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi has promised fellow European leaders wide-ranging changes to get Italy’s economy back on track, but it is not clear that he has the political clout to enact them.

2011-10-28.title: German Leader Seizes Initiative and Confounds Her Critics
2011-10-28.abstract: Angela Merkel, the chancellor of Germany, appeared to defy her detractors as she helped lead the nations of the euro currency zone to the most comprehensive deal yet.

2011-10-28.title: Making the Uneasy Transition From Prisoner to Celebrity
2011-10-28.abstract: Mohammed Musa Taqatqa, among the first batch of Palestinian prisoners freed in return for an Israeli tank soldier, is trying to adjust to his new life.

2011-10-28.title: Brazilian Amazon Groups Invade Site of Dam Project
2011-10-28.abstract: About 300 members of local indigenous groups invaded the Belo Monte dam site in Pará, where a workers’ camp is being built for the dam.

2011-10-29.title: 40 Killed Across Syria in Deadliest Friday Demonstrations Since May
2011-10-29.abstract: Syrian opposition groups have asked for international aid, though only demonstrators went so far as to call for a no-fly zone.

2011-10-29.title: Car Bomb in Aden Kills Yemeni Counterterrorism Chief
2011-10-29.abstract: Maj. Ali al-Haji was killed in Aden, in southern Yemen, which has not seen as much of the urban warfare that is common in the capital, Sana.

2011-10-29.title: Mexico: 20 Killed in Shootouts
2011-10-29.abstract: Twenty people were killed Friday in multiple shootouts in two states, law enforcement officials said.

2011-10-29.title: Italy: Emergency Declared in Flood Zones
2011-10-29.abstract: The Italian government declared a state of emergency on Friday in areas struck by flooding and mud slides earlier this week, and allocated $92 million to assist residents there.

2011-10-29.title: Joy in Russia as a Symbol of the Arts Reopens
2011-10-29.abstract: As Russia’s elite took part in the Bolshoi Theater’s opening gala, hundreds waited outside on a cold, miserable night for a glimpse of “Swan Lake” on two large screens.

2011-10-30.title: Newswallah: Longreads Edition
2011-10-30.abstract: A review of long-form journalism across India during the past week.

2011-10-30.title: Floodwalls Keep Bangkok Dry but Provinces Angry
2011-10-30.abstract: By building a wall of sandbags to spare the low-lying Thai capital, in the delta of the country’s main river system, officials have sacrificed the provinces to the north.

2011-10-30.title: India Wins, as Vettel Leads From Start to Finish
2011-10-30.abstract: Defying all expectations of failure, India staged its inaugural Grand Prix without a serious glitch, while Sebastian Vettel won, taking his 11th race of the season.

2011-10-30.title: Russian Aide Says Issues With Georgia on Joining Trade Group May Be Settled Soon
2011-10-30.abstract: The adviser indicated that the obstacles to Russia joining the World Trade Organization could be resolved in a matter of hours after 18 years of stop-and-go negotiations.

2011-10-30.title: Attacks on Foreigners in Capital Get Afghan Faction’s Message Across
2011-10-30.abstract: The Haqqani network is telling the world and, especially, the Afghan public, that it is willing and able to kill foreign troops.

2011-10-31.title: Political Shift Seen in Rally in Pakistan
2011-10-31.abstract: A rally of about 100,000 people represented what supporters and some analysts called the emergence of Imran Khan, a former cricket star, as a serious political challenger.

2011-10-31.title: Airport in Yemen Is Hit by Shells as Attacks in the Capital Widen
2011-10-31.abstract: For the first time since fighting broke out in the Yemeni capital, Sana International Airport came under direct attack.

2011-10-31.title: After Attacks, Efforts to Restore Truce Between Israel and Groups in Gaza
2011-10-31.abstract: Tensions simmered as Egypt tried to restore an informal truce in Gaza and Israeli forces continued airstrikes in response to Palestinian rocket attacks.

2011-10-31.title: Hopes for Peace as Kyrgyzstan Votes for New President
2011-10-31.abstract: Almazbek Atambayev, a former prime minister, was poised to become president of Kyrgyzstan by voters seeking an end to the ethnic and regional turmoil that erupted in violence last year.

2011-10-31.title: American Identified as Bomber in Attack on African Union in Somalia
2011-10-31.abstract: An Islamist militia in Somalia said that Abdisalan Hussein Ali, who spent his formative years in Minneapolis, was one of two bombers in a suicide attack in Mogadishu.
